explanations.3: basename(__FILE__) extracts just the filename from the full path — so if the file is at /home/user/script.php, this gives you 'script.php'.
explanations.4: dirname(__FILE__) does the opposite of basename — it gives you the directory path without the filename.
explanations.8: RecursiveDirectoryIterator creates an object that can walk through a folder and all its subfolders to access files.
explanations.10: RecursiveIteratorIterator::CHILD_FIRST means process the deepest files and folders first before moving up — this is important because you can't delete a folder until it's empty.
explanations.11: The check for '.' and '..' prevents trying to delete the current directory and parent directory shortcuts, which would cause errors.
